card.intuition: Storing copies of web resources (like images, scripts, and CSS) locally in the browser so they don't have to be downloaded again on repeat visits.
card.analogy: Keeping a local copy of a map: the first time you visit a city, you buy a map (download). Next time you go back, you just pull the map out of your backpack (cache) instead of finding a store and buying it again.
when_to_use: Improving load times for repeat visitors
Reducing bandwidth costs for the server
Handling offline scenarios (when combined with Service Workers)
gotchas: Stale Cache: if you update your CSS file on the server but the user has a cached version, they won't see your changes until the cache expires or you change the filename (Cache Busting)
HTTP/1.1 'Cache-Control' headers are more powerful than the old 'Expires' headers

exercise.prompt: Identify the HTTP header used to mark a specific version of a file to check if it has changed (304 Not Modified).
exercise.starter_code:
def check_file_version():
    # The server sends an ________ (Entity Tag) to the browser
    header = "ETag"
    return header
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "etag"
assert check("ETag")
print("PASS")
